question: What does a yellow flag with red stripes indicate in Formula 1?

question: What is the function of the virtual safety car in Formula 1?
last_answer: The virtual safety car (VSC) in Formula 1 is used to neutralize the race in cases where it is necessary to show a double yellow flag due to a risk for competitors or track marshals, without the incident requiring the appearance of the safety car.  [Source: "In 2015, the use of the virtual safety car began to be implemented. It is used to neutralise the race in cases where it is necessary to show a double yellow flag in some area of the track due to the existence of a risk for competitors or track marshals, without the incident requiring the appearance of the safety car."]  When the VSC is initiated, all luminous panels around the circuit will display the letters VSC, and teams will be notified. Drivers must respect a minimum time established by the FIA at least once at each control post.  [Source: "When race control initiates the virtual safety car procedure, all luminous panels around the circuit will display the letters VSC and the teams will be notified. Drivers must respect a minimum time established by the FIA at least once at each control post."]  The VSC signals will be switched off and green lights will be activated to restart the race immediately, 10-15 seconds after the VSC period ends.  [Source: "To end the virtual safety car period, teams will be notified and at any point between 10 and 15 seconds later the VSC signals on the panels will be switched off and green lights will be activated to restart the race immediately."]  The VSC is used to indicate that the safety car is intervening on the track, and drivers must reduce speed, not overtake, and even be prepared to stop and change their line as a danger totally or partially obstructs the track.  [Source: "The safety car flag, which is a yellow flag with the letters SC or with a VSC marking on the panels, indicates that the safety car is intervening on the track. Drivers must reduce speed, not overtake, and even be prepared to stop and change their line as a danger totally or partially obstructs the track."]  The VSC is currently not widely used, as in these situations drivers are shown the information on the car screens if they are not segmented, and the driver is usually informed by radio.  [Source: "Currently it is not widely used, as in these situations drivers are shown the information on the car screens if they are not segmented, and the driver is usually informed by radio."]
